title: Volume
summary: Specializes cutoff functionals to renormalized PE volume and its anomaly.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specializes cutoff functionals to renormalized PE volume and its anomaly.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Ambient/PE/Renormalization/Volume.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Cutoff-volume data for one PE normal form. Its mathematical data are: normal Form: Poincare Normal Form Data. u, v, w X; cutoff Volume: Pos Real maps to Real; volume Expansion: Laurent Log Expansion Real.

Volume as an instance of the generic cutoff-functional interface. In explicit terms, for x of type pespace. u, v, its defining expression is cutoff Value : equals the function sending D to D.cutoff Volume expansion : equals the function sending D to D.volume Expansion.
correspondence.leanType: def volumeCutoffFunctional (X : PESpace.{u, v}) : CutoffFunctional (PEVolumeCutoffData.{u, v, w} X) Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The renormalized volume, defined as the constant Laurent coefficient. In explicit terms, for x of type pespace, and d of type pevolume cutoff data x, its defining expression is volume Cutoff Functional X.renormalized Value D.
correspondence.leanType: def RenormalizedVolume {X : PESpace} (D : PEVolumeCutoffData X) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The logarithmic volume anomaly. In explicit terms, for x of type pespace, and d of type pevolume cutoff data x, its defining expression is volume Cutoff Functional X.anomaly Value D.
correspondence.leanType: def VolumeAnomaly {X : PESpace} (D : PEVolumeCutoffData X) : Real

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For X of type PESpace, and D of type PEVolume Cutoff Data X, Renormalized Volume D equals finite Part D.volume Expansion.
correspondence.leanType: theorem RenormalizedVolume_eq_finitePart {X : PESpace} (D : PEVolumeCutoffData X) : RenormalizedVolume D = finitePart D.volumeExpansion

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For X of type PESpace, and D of type PEVolume Cutoff Data X, Volume Anomaly D equals log Anomaly D.volume Expansion.
correspondence.leanType: theorem VolumeAnomaly_eq_logAnomaly {X : PESpace} (D : PEVolumeCutoffData X) : VolumeAnomaly D = logAnomaly D.volumeExpansion

references.locator: Proposition 3.7, Proposition 3.10, Lemma 4.1, and Theorems 1.1/1.4 as applicable
references.note: Primary source for the renormalized-curvature-integral mechanism represented by these modules.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
